findings.description: The NC Waste Management logo appears slightly different in rendering between PDF and HTML, but is present and correctly placed in both.
findings.evidence: PDF shows the logo with specific styling; HTML shows the same logo in approximately the same position.

findings.description: The PDF header layout has officials' names (Pat McCrory, Donald R. Van Der Vaart, Michael Scott) right-aligned in the upper right corner alongside the logo on the left. In the HTML, these names are rendered below the logo in a left-aligned vertical stack, collapsing the two-column header layout.
findings.evidence: PDF: Logo left, names right-aligned. HTML: Logo on top, names listed below in left-aligned block.

findings.description: The digital signature block is present in the HTML version but not visible in the PDF version's first page rendering.
findings.evidence: HTML shows 'Digitally signed by Elizabeth S Werner' with DN and Date details. PDF page 1 does not show this information visually (it may be embedded metadata).

findings.description: The footer information from page 1 of the PDF (State of North Carolina address and phone) appears between pages in the HTML, which is acceptable for HTML conversion but slightly disrupts the page flow.
findings.evidence: PDF has footer at bottom of page 1. HTML shows it as inline content between sections.

findings.description: The first page content (memorandum header, purpose, and outline) is effectively duplicated. The HTML contains a first rendering within a <header> and initial content block, then the same content appears again more fully rendered in the <main> section, mirroring both the cover page and the detailed page 1 of the PDF.
findings.evidence: The memorandum date, To/From/Subject fields, introductory paragraph about the March 16 meeting, and the outline (I-IV) appear once in the PDF but the HTML renders them with overlapping structure: the header block contains official names, then the main block repeats the full memo content including the same outline list.

findings.description: The document uses h1 for 'MEMORANDUM' and h2 for sections I through IV. While acceptable, there is a nested <main> inside <main> which is a structural HTML issue, though not a heading text issue per se.
findings.evidence: HTML has <main> wrapping the whole document and another <main> inside it containing the actual content.

findings.description: The digital signature block includes the email domain 'ncdenr.gov' which appears in the PDF's embedded digital signature metadata. This is not fabricated content but rather a faithful reproduction of the signature metadata visible in the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'email=elizabeth.werner@nc denr.gov' in signature block; HTML shows 'email=elizabeth.werner@ncdenr.gov' - minor formatting difference only.

findings.description: In the Well Testing bullet point, the PDF contains the grammatical error 'test there wells' which the HTML faithfully preserves. This is correct behavior (not altering source text), noted for completeness.
findings.evidence: Both PDF and HTML: 'Speakers were frustrated Duke wouldn't test there wells if they were located outside the 1500-ft boundary.'
